audienceSections.normal: This chapter teaches [[grammar:zodra + subordinate clause]] and [[grammar:veel/weinig/genoeg + mass or plural noun]]. Use [[grammar:zodra]] for as soon as. Use [[grammar:veel]], [[grammar:weinig]], or [[grammar:genoeg]] before a mass noun or plural noun.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter introduces [[grammar:zodra + subordinate clause]]: [[grammar:Zodra]] introduces a temporal subordinate clause with verb-final order and immediate sequencing.; [[grammar:veel/weinig/genoeg + mass or plural noun]]: These quantifiers do not require an article and are compatible with mass or plural reference.

audienceSections.normal:
#### zodra + subordinate clause

Use [[grammar:zodra]] for as soon as. Example: [[grammar:Zodra de levering komt, controleert Eva de flessen.]]

#### veel/weinig/genoeg + mass or plural noun

Use [[grammar:veel]], [[grammar:weinig]], or [[grammar:genoeg]] before a mass noun or plural noun. Example: [[grammar:Er is genoeg bloem voor het brood.]]
audienceSections.expert:
#### zodra + subordinate clause

[[grammar:Zodra]] introduces a temporal subordinate clause with verb-final order and immediate sequencing. Literal example: [[grammar:Zodra de levering komt, controleert Eva de flessen.]]

#### veel/weinig/genoeg + mass or plural noun

These quantifiers do not require an article and are compatible with mass or plural reference. Literal example: [[grammar:Er is genoeg bloem voor het brood.]]

audienceSections.normal: [[grammar:Zodra]] signals immediate sequence. [[grammar:Veel]], [[grammar:weinig]], and [[grammar:genoeg]] combine naturally with mass nouns and plural count nouns.
audienceSections.expert: [[grammar:Zodra]] signals immediate sequence. [[grammar:Veel]], [[grammar:weinig]], and [[grammar:genoeg]] combine naturally with mass nouns and plural count nouns.

breakdown.normal:
- [[grammar:Zodra de levering komt, controleert Eva de flessen.]] — As soon as the delivery arrives, Eva checks the bottles.
- [[grammar:Er zijn veel flessen water en weinig flessen sap.]] — There are many bottles of water and few bottles of juice.
- [[grammar:Eva meet één liter melk voor de keuken.]] — Eva measures one liter of milk for the kitchen.
- [[grammar:Lotte ziet dat er weinig suiker is.]] — Lotte sees that there is little sugar.
- [[grammar:Er is genoeg bloem voor het brood.]] — There is enough flour for the bread.
- [[grammar:Milan brengt veel appels naar de voorraadkamer.]] — Milan takes many apples to the stockroom.
- [[grammar:Zodra Eva de lijst afrondt, belt Eva voor een nieuwe levering.]] — As soon as Eva finishes the list, Eva calls about a new delivery.
- [[grammar:De familie heeft genoeg koffie voor honderd kopjes.]] — The family has enough coffee for one hundred cups.
- [[grammar:Er is weinig ruimte in de kast, daarom ordent Milan de dozen.]] — There is little space in the cupboard, so Milan organizes the boxes.
- [[grammar:Zodra de voorraad klaar is, opent Eva het café.]] — As soon as the stock is ready, Eva opens the café.

- [[grammar:Er zijn veel flessen water en weinig flessen sap.]] — Literal reading evidence at Narrative > paragraph 1 > sentence 2.
- [[grammar:Eva meet één liter melk voor de keuken.]] — Literal reading evidence at Narrative > paragraph 1 > sentence 3.
- [[grammar:Lotte ziet dat er weinig suiker is.]] — Literal reading evidence at Narrative > paragraph 1 > sentence 4.
- [[grammar:Er is genoeg bloem voor het brood.]] — Literal reading evidence at Narrative > paragraph 2 > sentence 1.
- [[grammar:Milan brengt veel appels naar de voorraadkamer.]] — Literal reading evidence at Narrative > paragraph 2 > sentence 2.
- [[grammar:Zodra Eva de lijst afrondt, belt Eva voor een nieuwe levering.]] — Literal reading evidence at Narrative > paragraph 2 > sentence 3.
- [[grammar:De familie heeft genoeg koffie voor honderd kopjes.]] — Literal reading evidence at Narrative > paragraph 3 > sentence 1.
- [[grammar:Er is weinig ruimte in de kast, daarom ordent Milan de dozen.]] — Literal reading evidence at Narrative > paragraph 3 > sentence 2.
- [[grammar:Zodra de voorraad klaar is, opent Eva het café.]] — Literal reading evidence at Narrative > paragraph 3 > sentence 3.
